preamble: The close front unrounded vowel [i] lowers to the open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] through vowel lowering. This process reduces vowel height while preserving frontness and rounding features. The shift is documented in historical sound change patterns and sociolinguistic variation.

examples.note: Lowering in unstressed syllables and pre-consonantal contexts, particularly in casual speech.
Regional variation affecting vowel quality in certain German dialects.
Allophonic lowering of high front vowels in certain phonological contexts.
Reduction in casual speech and rapid articulation.
